title: Treasury Department Designates BNY as Financial Agent to Support New Trump Accounts Program
summary: The U.S. Department of the Treasury designated The Bank of New York Mellon Corporation (BNY) as a financial agent of the U.S. government to support implementation of the new Trump Accounts program, a savings/investment platform for eligible children. BNY has partnered with Robinhood, which will serve as brokerage and initial trustee for the accounts.
key_points: BNY designated as financial agent of the U.S. government for Trump Accounts program
Robinhood partnered with BNY to serve as brokerage and initial trustee
Trump Accounts app is a custom white-label product being developed exclusively for Treasury
National Design Studio, in conjunction with Robinhood, is creating the user interface and experience
Treasury will retain control over the app and operations for initial accounts
Treasury has longstanding statutory authority to designate qualified financial institutions as financial agents
why_it_matters: This announcement establishes the operational infrastructure for a new government savings/investment program targeting families with children. The designation of BNY as financial agent and partnership with Robinhood represents a public-private arrangement in government financial services delivery. However, this is an administrative announcement with no direct implications for monetary policy, interest rates, or broader market structure.
market_structure_context: The designation represents a financial agency relationship between Treasury and BNY for fiduciary services. The partnership with Robinhood as brokerage introduces a retail brokerage into a government financial agency arrangement. Treasury's statutory authority to designate financial agents is noted but not elaborated. No changes to Treasury's operating framework, market operations, or market plumbing are described.
